text: Yay! Happy Halloween. Happy Halloween,
everyone. This is extra special because
this is Wes's first Halloween. So, we
are so excited to take him
trick-or-treating for the first time.
He's still a little young, so
trick-or-treating might not go as
planned, but we're at least going to
walk around the neighborhood and let him
people watch and see everybody in their
costumes. This brings back a lot of
memories for me of getting dressed up
with my sister, running around our
neighborhood, getting candy, and staying
out a little bit later, and then coming
home and sorting it all. In America, the
traditions can vary greatly, but most of
the time, people will hand out candy.
And if your front porch light is on,
that means you have candy and you're
handing it out. If it's off, that either
means you ran out of candy or you are
not home. So, we are going to have a lot
of fun eating all of Wes's candy
apparently. So, come get ready for our
first Halloween with us. Wes was going
to dress up as Chucky the demon child,
but he outgrew his overalls. So, we are
going to wear a Halloween themed shirt
this year, and next year we'll get him a
proper costume. There you go.
And his final costume.
We had some trouble getting it over his
head because honestly, I have never put

always went from the bottom up. But we
are ready in our costume. We're going to
put some pants on and then we're going
to go trick-or-treating.
Yeah. Oh, we're excited. So, before we
head out for trick-or-treating, I am
going to feed him his dinner of chicken
and green beans. In my town, when you
hear the tornado siren going off, that
means trick-or-treating has started.
It'll start at 6:00 p.m. and then it'll
go off again at 8:00 p.m., meaning that
the trickor treat is over. But we also
want to make sure that we are packing up
our stroller. I always have an emergency
kit for us that includes wipes, diapers,
and one extra onesie. [snorts] What was
